subject: Intro: Vectrix Labs // Luminary Health Engineering

messages.subject: Intro: Vectrix Labs // Luminary Health Engineering
messages.body_text:
Priya, Chloe — happy to connect you both.

Priya Chandran is the CEO of Vectrix Labs, which I've invested in. They build API observability tooling — endpoint-level latency tracking, anomaly detection, alerting. They're currently working with healthcare clients (Meridian Health Systems is in their pipeline) and have addressed the data residency and SOC 2 questions that come up in regulated-industry deals.

Chloe Andrews is CTO of Luminary Health, which provides remote patient monitoring software. Chloe — Vectrix may be a fit for the API reliability visibility needs we discussed last week.

I'll leave it to you two from here!

— Sarah Lindgren
Angel Investor
sarah.lindgren@angelinvestor.com

messages.subject: Re: Intro: Vectrix Labs // Luminary Health Engineering
messages.body_text:
Hi Chloe,

Wonderful to meet you — thank you Sarah for the intro.

A quick orientation: Vectrix gives your engineering team endpoint-level visibility into your production APIs — latency percentiles (P50/P95/P99), anomaly detection, and smart alerting to Slack or PagerDuty. Setup is typically 45 minutes with our SDK. For healthcare teams specifically: all data stays in AWS US regions, we hold SOC 2 Type I, and we support SAML SSO via Clerk.

Would a 20-minute call this week or next work? I'm looping in Tessa Moreno, our Head of Sales, to help coordinate.

— Priya Chandran
CEO, Vectrix Labs
priya@vectrix.io

Hi Priya,

Nice to meet you. Sarah mentioned you're working with Meridian — if you're navigating healthcare API compliance requirements you've probably already solved the things we'd ask about.

— Chloe Andrews

messages.subject: Checking in — Q1 trajectory
messages.body_text:
Hi Priya,

Great board call last week. A few thoughts after sleeping on it:

1. Meridian: the SOC 2 Type II timeline is the thing I'd watch most carefully. If their legal team makes it a contract condition precedent — not just a commitment — that's a 6-month blocker. Do you have a sense of how firm Rachel Simmons is on that?

2. Dashboard v2: make sure Cascade and Brainloop have both seen a demo before the public launch. Reference customers who've only seen the old product are a missed opportunity.

3. Hiring: the Sr. Frontend search is taking longer than I'd like. Is comp the issue?

Happy to be helpful however I can — just ping me.

— David Kim
Angel Investor
david.kim@angelinvestor.com
messages.body_html:

2. Dashboard v2: make sure Cascade and Brainloop have both seen a demo before the public launch. Reference customers who've only seen the old product are a missed opportunity.

— David Kim

david.kim@angelinvestor.com

messages.subject: Re: Checking in — Q1 trajectory
messages.body_text:
David,

All three are fair observations.

1. Rachel Simmons has been reasonable — she's accepted the June 30 Type II commitment in the questionnaire responses. The protection she wants is a termination-for-cause right if we miss Sept 30. That's in the Orion Legal redlines. I think it's acceptable.

2. Dashboard v2 demos for Cascade and Brainloop — I'll have Tessa set those up in the week before launch. Good call.

3. Hiring: yes, comp was the issue — Derek Huang declined at $145K, accepted $160K elsewhere. We've raised the band to $145K–$165K. Anya Chen is our lead candidate now, final panel is Feb 27. Strong signals from the take-home.

Thanks for staying close on this.

— Priya Chandran
CEO, Vectrix Labs
priya@vectrix.io
